# Questions in Livestream 79

Live Stream #79: Another Vague Cleopatra Tomb Sighting

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=371" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:11</a> | Why does Balaam not seem to react more strongly when his donkey talks in Numbers 22? | He said the text never says Balaam did not react, and a prophet already accustomed to strange divine encounters may not find one more miracle shocking. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=726" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:06</a> | Which parts of his Ark of the Covenant book does he most recommend? | He especially recommended chapter 6, with chapter 1 as a strong short introduction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=997" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:37</a> | Is Deuteronomy 4:24 an example of Zoroastrian influence? | He said the chronology is wrong for Zoroastrian influence on Deuteronomy, though later books such as Daniel or Ezra-Nehemiah could raise different questions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=1199" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:59</a> | What is life? | He reframed the question theologically: life is connection to God, Christ, the Spirit, other people, and creation, while separation from God is death. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=1459" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:19</a> | Does Paul's use of Plato mean the Bible accepts Platonism or Plato's philosophical and political ideas? | No. He said biblical authors can quote or engage correct ideas from outside sources without endorsing the whole source. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=1767" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:27</a> | Why do Orthodox Jews call Jesus "Yeshu" rather than "Yeshua," and which name best represents how he would have been addressed in his time? | He treated "Yeshu" and "Yeshua" as abbreviated forms and said Jesus would have been called something like Yahushua in the first century. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=2012" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:32</a> | Did Nebuchadnezzar set up a canopy at Tahpanhes as in Jeremiah 43:9-10, and did he take spoil from Egypt or Elephantine as in Ezekiel 29:19? | He said the canopy may refer to a war tent or base of operations, and he is more confident that Babylonian forces took spoil from Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=2547" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:27</a> | What does he think of UsefulCharts saying Moses existed but the Exodus was a tale and there was no conquest? | He said there is evidence relevant to conquest claims, including Hazor, Jericho, Mount Ebal, and early Israelite settlement, and called the view odd. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=2745" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:45</a> | What does he think about the growing rift between biblical ancient Near Eastern studies and theology departments? | He said the split is real and is often worsened by institutional finances and the dominance of New Testament priorities in seminaries. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=3464" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:44</a> | Is the Canaanite comb found at Tel Lachish as revolutionary as its finders say? | He said it is interesting evidence for writing and Egypt-Canaan trade around 1700 BC, but he would wait for peer-reviewed publication before calling it revolutionary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=4095" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:15</a> | When does he date the life of David, and does he know good scholars on the Davidic and First Temple periods? | He tentatively placed David around 1008-996 BC, with rough dating, and said he would need to look up current specialist recommendations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=4276" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:16</a> | Was Israel unusual among ancient cultures for recording many of its own failures? | Yes. He said Israel was exceptionally unusual because its religious institutions could critique the civil monarchy in a way other Near Eastern royal systems did not. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=4671" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:51</a> | Would Mosaic authorship be unlikely because the Torah reflects multilingual law, history, finance, culture, and literary skill despite low literacy and limited document access? | No. He argued Moses' elite Egyptian setting would have given him scribal training and access to administrative, legal, literary, and multilingual materials. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=5186" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:26</a> | Why do the apostles often preach Christ from less obvious Old Testament texts rather than clear messianic prophecies such as Isaiah 53? | He suggested they chose texts tailored to their arguments and that using more obscure passages reflects careful, literate, methodical writing rather than ignorance of obvious texts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=5714" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:14</a> | Did the Magi use astrology to find Yeshua, is astrology prohibited in the Bible, and why were the Magi not condemned? | He said they almost certainly used a codified ancient form of astrology; it was not condoned for Israel, but the Magi were pagans whom God used without first correcting every practice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=6352" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:52</a> | Who from church history has too little written about them and potential for more research? | He mentioned Chrysostom, Ambrose, Japanese martyrs, and Julius Africanus as possible subjects, depending on source availability and prior coverage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=6682" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:22</a> | Does Numbers 33:3 mentioning Ramesses and Numbers 13:22 mentioning Zoan prove scribes did not redact toponyms, and is Zoan the best anachronism? | He said Zoan is likely an editorial gloss, while Ramesses being left intact suggests scribes added explanatory notes more than they redacted the underlying text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=7221" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:21</a> | Is Cameron Bertuzzi's conversion to Catholicism part of a broader trend among intellectual Protestants, and has Thomism caused controversy in seminaries? | He said the trend is happening more, partly because of Protestant failures, but he was more concerned with Bertuzzi's stated reasons than the conversion itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=8106" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:06</a> | What about wax tablets? | He described wax tablets as reusable ancient note-taking devices, often used for temporary notes that could later be copied onto papyrus for permanent storage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=8535" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:15</a> | Is eschatology a waste of time? | He said studying eschatology is not a waste, but an excessive focus that displaces the rest of Scripture is spiritually unhealthy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=8779" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:19</a> | Is the Egyptian myth about Ra creating the sun and moon and then slapping the moon with a rabbit genuine? | He said the eyes of Ra element reflects a late Egyptian myth, but the rabbit element appears to be a later or cross-cultural mixture, not Egyptian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=9220" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:40</a> | Would he consider doing a series or shorts about spiritual junk food? | Yes. He said a video or follow-up series on spiritual junk food could fit his earlier critique of shallow apologetics and church teaching. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=10370" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:50</a> | What was Bach's favorite instrument, or which instrument did he start with? | He did not know and said he is not a music historian; he mainly plays Bach from sheet music on guitar. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=10477" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:37</a> | Did Egyptian religion treat its gods as gender-fluid? | No. He said Egyptian deities had fixed genders, even when some iconography borrowed attributes associated with the opposite sex. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=10734" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:54</a> | Is Esther a pagan name? | No. He said it is not related to Ishtar, but is a Semitic name connected with myrtle and is not theophoric. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/xPyCgeAk_F8?t=10940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:20</a> | What technical field is most useful for someone who wants to work in history, archaeology, or languages? | He recommended programming first, with database work as another especially useful technical skill for archaeology and digital humanities. |
